_note: Strict, recipe-owned spec read by c-eval-runner. A brand may layer brand-overrides/<slug>/acceptance.json to TIGHTEN thresholds (same id wins; new ids appended). See c-eval-runner/SKILL.md for the spec format.

checks.desc: Mechanical gate (loudness ≈ -14 LUFS, frame-0 brightness, resolution 1080×1920, fps, audio present) delegated to c-shorts-qa-gate.
AI character short: 30–60s nominal; 27–63s allows ±3s for crossfade trim and encode rounding.
Exact 9:16 portrait canvas — scenes must be scaled to 1080×1920 before assembly.
Center zone (middle 960px of height) must have YAVG ≥ 16 at every sample — ensures AI-generated scenes have visible content and none of the animated clips failed to render.

(a) Character maintains all 6 locked attributes across every scene — face shape, hair, eyes, clothing, distinctive feature, and color palette do not drift between scenes. The 6-attribute lock audit must be satisfied from Step 0.

(b) No diverged character frames — the character looks identical to char-ref across all scenes; any frame where face, outfit, or distinctive feature has changed is a defect that must be regenerated before delivery.
(c) Scene crossfades are smooth and natural — no jump cuts or abrupt hard-cuts between animated clips; the crossfade duration parameter is visibly present at each scene boundary.
(d) The character occupies a substantial portion of each scene frame — not a tiny figure lost in the environment; full body or upper-body close-up fills the composition.
(e) Frame 0 (cover) shows the character clearly in a compelling composition — not black, not mid-crossfade, not a blurred or partially-loaded frame.
Assembled composite must be full portrait canvas before delivery.
Center zone of assembled composite must have visible content at beginning, middle, and end — catches a failed scene clip that produced a black segment.
